summary: solana-web3-community is a typosquat of @solana/web3.js with hardcoded attacker RPC and Telegram C2 exfiltration
details: The package impersonates @solana/web3.js (name 'solana-web3-community', author 'Solana Labs Maintainers <maintainers@solanalabs.com>', repository pointing at solana-foundation/solana-web3.js) and ships a tampered bundle. The first ~11,150 lines of lib/index.cjs.js (and the parallel lib/index.esm.js, lib/index.iife.js, lib/index.iife.min.js, lib/index.native.js, lib/index.browser.cjs.js) reproduce the legitimate @solana/web3.js Rollup output, with malicious code appended after line 11200. The injected payload hardcodes a rogue Solana RPC at http://104.239.66.223:8899 (cleartext HTTP, referenced multiple times) wired into the Connection/Transaction code path so that callers who instantiate `new Connection(...)` and call `sendTransaction` have signed transactions and key material diverted to the attacker's RPC. The same payload also POSTs captured material to https://api.telegram.org/bot<redacted>/sendMessage (Telegram bot C2), and uses child_process + curl primitives for additional exfiltration shell-outs. Any developer who installs this package as a community fork of @solana/web3.js will have wallet operations silently routed through the attacker, enabling direct theft of installer-controlled funds. The append-after-legit-bundle shape is a deliberate evasion of casual diff review.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
